## Overview
Please read carefully the following “Document requirement”, “Photo Specifications”, “Download Form” to properly prepare your application in conformity with the specific requirements of the Decision marker for this visa category.

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fee for the applicable visa type is listed in the table below. The fee mentioned is applicable per applicant. 

[Table:  Study visa]

## Service fee

[Table:  Study visa]
Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in Vietnamese Dong (VND) is as per current exchange rate and will be updated later. It is subject to change without notice.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application Centre can pay the visa fee in cash or through credit / debit card.
- Applicants exempted from visa fee need to pay only VFS Global Service fee.
- The service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please refer to document checklist for you visa application here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/checklist-scientists-and-researchers-Jan-25.pdf).
As a rule, applicants must submit visa applications, together with all necessary documents, in person at the Visa Application Centre. In order to avoid time-consuming requests for additional information or documentation, applicants should consult the VFS’s websites and the website of the respective mission well in advance of their departure date to find out about the visa procedure and about the documentation which has to be submitted.
Visa application forms can be downloaded from the VFS’s website. The forms submitted must be original versions in the appropriate language of the mission in question.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Other requirements:
- No staples on any documents
- Original documents + 1 sets of two sides photocopied documents.
- Documents in Vietnamese must be accompanied with the translation in German.

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
Photo specifications guideline
Requirements:
- Should NOT be older than 6 months and without Photoshop touching
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-36mm) of the photo;
- A uniform and white-colored background;
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame;
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons;
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form.

## Processing Time
The processing time for all the categories for long-term visas is from 4 to 8 weeks.

## Download Forms
Below is the form that is required to be filled to submit your visa application:
- National Visa Application Form (To be filled online - mandatory): The National Visa Application form must be filled in English. Please click here (https://videx-national.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/#/videx-langfristiger-aufenthalt).

- Once complete print out the form on clear paper. It is recommended to save the application form under the . JSON file format and keep it on your email, allowing a quick and easy information correction or update if it requires.
- The complete the printed set of your application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application.
- The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and must be printed in high resolution in black and white. The printer must be set as below:

visa_types.name: Medical treatment
visa_types.documents_required:
Please refer to document checklist for you visa application here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/Checklist-for-medical-recent.pdf).
As a rule, applicants must submit visa applications, together with all necessary documents, in person at the Visa Application Centre responsible for their place of residence. In order to avoid time-consuming requests for additional information or documentation, applicants should consult the VFS’s websites and the website of the respective mission well in advance of their departure date to find out about the visa procedure and about the documentation which has to be submitted.
Visa application forms can be downloaded from the VFS’s website. The forms submitted must be original versions in the appropriate language of the mission in question.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Important note:
- Photocopies that have been authenticated by Vietnamese  government offices or notaries cannot be accepted for submission in lieu of  original documents. However, it is permissible to submit, in lieu of originals,  photocopies that have been recently (within the last 3 months) authenticated: 
- by the German Embassy in Hanoi;
- by the German Consulate General in Ho Chi Minh City;
- by any German government office or
- by any Notary Public in Germany.
For a better understanding about the authentication process, please  refer to the German Embassy and Consulate General’ website at https://vietnam.diplo.de/vn-vi/service/c%C3%B4ng-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-b%E1%BA%A3n-sao-v%C3%A0-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-th%E1%BB%B1c-ch%E1%BB%AF-k%C3%BD/2195386 (https://vietnam.diplo.de/vn-vi/service/c%C3%B4ng-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-b%E1%BA%A3n-sao-v%C3%A0-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-th%E1%BB%B1c-ch%E1%BB%AF-k%C3%BD/2195386)
- We strongly recommend the applicant to arrange your application per the checklist’s order, avoiding the delayed submission.
- Travel  insurance: 
- The policy is valid worldwide  or in the Schengen (Schengen Area) or Europe;
- The policy covers the areas  required by the visa code:
- Medical expenses (inpatient and outpatient)
- (Medical) evacuation
- Repatriation (of mortal remains)
- The insured sum for all these  is equal to or higher than 30,000 EUR
- There is no sub-limit on these  that is lower than 30,000 EUR.
Applicant is obliged to provide all information to the best of  his or her knowledge and belief according to the regulation in Section 54 (2) 8  in conjunction with Section 53 of the Residence Act. Download form here and submit with the visa application form.
visa_types.visa_fees:
The Visa Fee for the applicable visa type is listed in the table below. The fee mentioned is applicable per applicant. 

[Table:  Medical treatment]
Fee waivers for Schengen visas: 
 Visa fees are waived by all Schengen states for the following categories of persons, regardless of their nationality: 
- Children under six years.
- School pupils, undergraduates, postgraduates and accompanying teaching staff who undertake trips for the purpose of study or training.
- Representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations.
- Researchers from third countries travelling within the European Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005
- For the “replacement” of an old, valid visa in a “full” travel document (one that has no empty pages) with a new visa valid for the same period in the applicant’s new travel document.
- Visa fees are waived for the spouses, same-sex partners in a civil union and minor single children of German citizens, the parents of minor German citizens and family members of EU/EEA nationals, provided they enjoy freedom of movement.
Reduced fees:
- The visa facilitation agreements with Armenia and Azerbaijan stipulate a fee of EUR 35.00 ~ 1.100.000 VND for a Schengen visa worldwide and various waivers of this visa fee (e.g. for visits to relatives).
- For those nationals of Serbia, the Former Yugoslav Republic of Macedonia, Montenegro, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Moldova, Albania, Ukraine and Georgia who still require a visa (holders of non-biometric passports), visa facilitation agreements stipulate a fee of EUR 35.00 ~ 1.100.000 VND.
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing of the application takes up to 15 working days.
The Embassy / the Consulate General of Germany in Vietnam may request further documentation, information or you may be called for an interview which can prolong the processing time.
After applying for your visa, you will not be able to borrow your passport back while your visa application is being processed.
If you need your passport during the visa process for other purposes, please inform us during the application process. You will have to hand in the passport at the same VfS-Center you applied as soon as possible to finalize the application process

- Schengen Visa Application Form (To be filled online - mandatory): The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled in English. Please click here (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/desktop/index.html).
- Declaration of consent for minors can be downloaded here.
- Declaration on travel insurance if you want to apply for multiple entries visa can be downloaded here.
- Declaration for visiting multiple Schengen countries can be downloaded here.
- Section 54 (2) 8 in conjunction with Section 53 of the Residence Act can be downloaded here.
Instructions to complete an online form:
- Click on the form link above;
- When the page opens complete the form. All the following fields are mandatory to fill up: 
- Issued by;
- Duration of the intended stay or transit; Intended date of arrival in the Schengen area;
- Intended date of the departure from the Schengen area.
- You should provide all the information, if possible, on VIDEX, even if the field is not obligatory.

- Children under six years.

visa_types.name: Schengen visa
visa_types.overview:
Schengen Rule
The visa application has to be presented to the Embassy/Consulate of the country of main destination (determined by the purpose of the journey and the length of stay). If your main destination cannot be ascertained, the Schengen state through which you first enter the territory of these countries is responsible for granting the visa.
Do I need a Schengen visa for Germany and Portugal?
In case you want to travel to several European countries which are part of the Schengen States you can only apply at the German Embassy or at the German Consulate General if your main destination or only destination is Germany. The German Embassy Hanoi and the German Consulate General in Ho Chi Minh city also processes applications for the main destination Portugal. 
              Click here (https://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/en/einreiseundaufenthalt/visabestimmungen-node/staatenlistevisumpflicht-node) to check if you require a Schengen visa to enter Germany and Portugal. Please note that applicant to Portugal can use the same checklist applicable for Germany. You can find the checklists in the section “Documents required” for every visa category.
Note: However, please note that applicants intending to travel to Portugal in order to receive a residence permit under the ‘Golden Visa program” (= ARI - Residence permit for investment activity) (https://www.vistos.mne.pt/en/ari-residency-permit-for-investors (https://www.vistos.mne.pt/en/ari-residency-permit-for-investors)) must submit their visa applications to the Embassy of Portugal in Bangkok. 
Please refer for further information by thislink (https://vietnam.diplo.de/vn-vi/dichvulanhsu/05-VisaEinreise/-/2504742?openAccordionId=item-2504742-1-panel)
Schengen Visa and Visa Code
  In order to apply for a Schengen visa, the applicant needs to provide the documents as per the purpose of the trip. In individual cases, additional documents may be requested. Presentation of the documents does not automatically lead to the issuance of a visa and possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen visa. The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
 The Visa Code standardizes the visa requirements which must be examined by the mission in the course of the visa procedure. The respective mission makes a decision on the visa application at its own discretion, taking into account all the circumstances in any given individual case.
All visa applicants for Germany are advised to schedule an appointment and visit the Joint Visa Application Centres in Vietnam to submit their applications, together with all necessary documents.
Processing time for a visa may take up to 15 working days. Applicants are therefore requested to organize themselves accordingly taking into consideration possible waiting periods due to the online appointment system.
The purpose of the proposed visit may not be altered once the visa has been issued. This is also applicable after entering the Schengen territory.
Above is regular information, if you wish to continue and to know more about the Visa application process, kindly Click here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/Germany/Vietnam/know-your-visa-type.html).
Information on this website is subject to change, kindly check the information for updates before you apply.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
It is possible to get a visa with the purpose of study or other types of training (maximum 90 Days of stay).
Please read carefully the following parts “Document requirement”, “Photo Specifications”, “Download Form” to properly prepare your application in conformity with the specific requirements of the Decision marker for this visa category.

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fee for the applicable visa type is listed in the table below. The fee mentioned is applicable per applicant. 

[Table:  Tourist ]
Fee waivers for Schengen visas:
Visa fees are waived by all Schengen states for the following categories of persons, regardless of their nationality:
- Children under six years.
- School pupils, undergraduates, postgraduates and accompanying teaching staff who undertake trips for the purpose of study or training.
- Representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations.
- Researchers from third countries travelling within the European Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005
- For the “replacement” of an old, valid visa in a “full” travel document (one that has no empty pages) with a new visa valid for the same period in the applicant’s new travel document.
- Visa fees are waived for the spouses, same-sex partners in a civil union and minor single children of German citizens, the parents of minor German citizens and family members of EU/EEA nationals, provided they enjoy freedom of movement.
Reduced fees:
- The visa facilitation agreements with Armenia and Azerbaijan stipulate a fee of EUR 35.00 ~ 1.100.000 VND for a Schengen visa worldwide and various waivers of this visa fee (e.g. for visits to relatives).
- For those nationals of Serbia, the Former Yugoslav Republic of Macedonia, Montenegro, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Moldova, Albania, Ukraine and Georgia who still require a visa (holders of non-biometric passports), visa facilitation agreements stipulate a fee of EUR 35.00 ~ 1.100.000 VND.

## Service fee

[Table:  Tourist ]
Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in Vietnamese Dong (VND) is as per current exchange rate and will be updated later. It is subject to change without notice.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application Centre can pay the visa fee in cash or through credit / debit card.
- Applicants exempted from visa fee need to pay only VFS Global Service fee.
- The service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please refer to document checklist for you visa application here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/Checklist-for-visa-applications-L.pdf).
As a rule, applicants must submit visa applications, together with all necessary documents, in person at the Visa Application Centre responsible for their place of residence. In order to avoid time-consuming requests for additional information or documentation, applicants should consult the VFS’s websites and the website of the respective mission well in advance of their departure date to find out about the visa procedure and about the documentation which has to be submitted.
Visa application forms can be downloaded from the VFS’s website. The forms submitted must be original versions in the appropriate language of the mission in question.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Important note:
- Photocopies that have been authenticated by Vietnamese government offices or notaries cannot be accepted for submission in lieu of original documents. However, it is permissible to submit, in lieu of originals, photocopies that have been recently (within the last 3 months) authenticated:
- by the German Embassy in Hanoi;
- by the German Consulate General in Ho Chi Minh City;
- by any German government office or
- by any Notary Public in Germany.
For a better understanding about the authentication process, please refer to the German Embassy and Consulate General’ website at https://vietnam.diplo.de/vn-vi/service/c%C3%B4ng-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-b%E1%BA%A3n-sao-v%C3%A0-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-th%E1%BB%B1c-ch%E1%BB%AF-k%C3%BD/2195386 (https://vietnam.diplo.de/vn-vi/service/c%C3%B4ng-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-b%E1%BA%A3n-sao-v%C3%A0-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-th%E1%BB%B1c-ch%E1%BB%AF-k%C3%BD/2195386)
- We strongly recommend the applicant to arrange your application per the checklist’s order, avoiding the delayed submission
Applicant is obliged to provide all information to the best of his or her knowledge and belief according to the regulation in Section 54 (2) 8 in conjunction with Section 53 of the Residence Act. Download form here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/Download-form.pdf) and submit with the visa application form.

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as  per specifications.
Photo specifications guideline
Requirements:
- Should NOT be older than 6 months and without Photoshop touching
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-36mm) of the photo;
- A uniform and white-colored background;
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame;
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons;
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form.

## Processing Time
The processing of the application takes up to 15 working days.
The Embassy / the Consulate General of Germany in Vietnam may request further documentation, information or you may be called for an interview which can prolong the processing time.
After applying for your visa, you will not be able to borrow your passport back while your visa application is being processed.
If you need your passport during the visa process for other purposes, please inform us during the application process. You will have to hand in the passport at the same VfS-Center you applied as soon as possible to finalize the application process

## Download Forms
Below is the form that is required to be filled to submit your visa application:
- Schengen Visa Application Form (To be filled online - mandatory): The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled in English. Please click here (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/desktop/index.html).
- Declaration of consent for minors can be downloaded here.
- Declaration on travel insurance if you want to apply for multiple entries visa can be downloaded here.
- Declaration for visiting multiple Schengen countries can be downloaded here.
- Section 54 (2) 8 in conjunction with Section 53 of the Residence Act can be downloaded here.
Instructions to complete an online form:
- Click on the form link above;
- When the page opens, complete the form. All the following fields are mandatory to fill up: 
- Issued by;
- Duration of the intended stay or transit; Intended date of arrival in the Schengen area;
- Intended date of the departure from the Schengen area.
- You should provide all the information, if possible, on VIDEX, even if the field is not obligatory.
- Once complete print out the form on clear paper. It is recommended to save the application form under the .JSON file format and keep it on your email, allowing a quick and easy information correction or update if it requires.
- The complete the printed set of your application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application.
- ** The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and must be printed in high resolution in black and white. The printer must be set as below:

## Overview
This visa is solely for the family members who accompany with applicants for employment visa (this includes employment with and without academical education as well as scientists or researchers – not job seekers!) Please read carefully the following “Document requirement”, “Photo Specifications”, “Download Form” to properly prepare your application in conformity with the specific requirements of the Decision marker for this visa category.

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fee for the applicable visa type is listed in the table below. The fee mentioned is applicable per applicant. 

[Table:  Study visa]

## Service fee

[Table:  Study visa]
Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in Vietnamese Dong (VND) is as per current exchange rate and will be updated later. It is subject to change without notice.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application Centre can pay the visa fee in cash or through credit / debit card.
- Applicants exempted from visa fee need to pay only VFS Global Service fee.
- The service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required

Please refer to document checklist for you visa application here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/checklist-accompanying-family-members-Feb-24.pdf).
As a rule, applicants must submit visa applications, together with all necessary documents, in person at the Visa Application Centre. In order to avoid time-consuming requests for additional information or documentation, applicants should consult the VFS’s websites and the website of the respective mission well in advance of their departure date to find out about the visa procedure and about the documentation which has to be submitted.
Visa application forms can be downloaded from the VFS’s website. The forms submitted must be original versions in the appropriate language of the mission in question.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Other requirements: 
- No staples on any documents
- Original documents + 1 sets of two sides photocopied documents.
- Documents in Vietnamese must be accompanied with the translation in German.
- The original of the contract is required, with a clear and readable stamp of the training company, signature and name of the person who signed.

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
Photo specifications guideline (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/photo-specification.pdf)
Requirements:
- Should NOT be older than 6 months and without Photoshop touching
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-36mm) of the photo;
- A uniform and white-colored background;
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame;
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons;
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form.

visa_types.overview:
National visa applications for the following categories can only be submitted through VFS: Study Visa, Visa for employment with academical education, Visa for Recognition of foreign educational achievements, Vocational Training visa, Visa for Employment without academical education, Visa for Scientists and researchers, Visa for Job seekers and vocational training seeker, Visa for Family members accompanying applicants for employment visa, Visa for professional drivers, Visa for employment with practical qualification and Specialty chefs.

Please note that in contrast some national visa applications can only be submitted at the German Embassy in Hanoi or the German Consulate General in Ho-Chi-Minh-City. To find out where you have to submit your national visa application please consult our website and/or the website  (https://vietnam.diplo.de/vn-vi/dichvulanhsu/05-VisaEinreise/-/2069630?isLocal=false&isPreview=false)of the German Missions in Vietnam. The processing time for all the below mentioned categories is 4 to 8 weeks.

Fee waivers for Schengen visas:
Visa fees are waived by all Schengen states for the following categories of persons, regardless of their nationality:
- Children under six years.
- School pupils, undergraduates, postgraduates and accompanying teaching staff who undertake trips for the purpose of study or training.
- Representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations.
- Researchers from third countries travelling within the European Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005
- For the “replacement” of an old, valid visa in a “full” travel document (one that has no empty pages) with a new visa valid for the same period in the applicant’s new travel document.
- Visa fees are waived for the spouses, same-sex partners in a civil union and minor single children of German citizens, the parents of minor German citizens and family members of EU/EEA nationals, provided they enjoy freedom of movement.
Reduced fees:
- The visa facilitation agreements with Armenia and Azerbaijan stipulate a fee of EUR 35.00 ~ 1.100.000 VND for a Schengen visa worldwide and various waivers of this visa fee (e.g. for visits to relatives).
- For those nationals of Serbia, the Former Yugoslav Republic of Macedonia, Montenegro, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Moldova, Albania, Ukraine and Georgia who still require a visa (holders of non-biometric passports), visa facilitation agreements stipulate a fee of EUR 35.00 ~ 1.100.000 VND.

Overview

Please read carefully the following “Document requirement”, “Photo Specifications”, “Download Form” to properly prepare your application in conformity with the specific requirements of the Decision marker for this visa category.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Applicants traveling to German to visit family or friends including girlfriend/boyfriend may apply for this visa.
Please read carefully the following “Document requirement”, “Photo Specifications”, “Download Form” to properly prepare your application in conformity with the specific requirements of the Decision marker for this visa category.

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fee for the applicable visa type is listed in the table below. The fee mentioned is applicable per applicant. 

[Table:  Tourist ]
Fee waivers for Schengen visas:
Visa fees are waived by all Schengen states for the following categories of persons, regardless of their nationality:
- Children under six years.
- School pupils, undergraduates, postgraduates and accompanying teaching staff who undertake trips for the purpose of study or training.
- Representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations.
- Researchers from third countries travelling within the European Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005
- For the “replacement” of an old, valid visa in a “full” travel document (one that has no empty pages) with a new visa valid for the same period in the applicant’s new travel document.
- Visa fees are waived for the spouses, same-sex partners in a civil union and minor single children of German citizens, the parents of minor German citizens and family members of EU/EEA nationals, provided they enjoy freedom of movement.
Reduced fees:
- The visa facilitation agreements with Armenia and Azerbaijan stipulate a fee of EUR 35.00 ~ 1.100.000 VND for a Schengen visa worldwide and various waivers of this visa fee (e.g. for visits to relatives).
- For those nationals of Serbia, the Former Yugoslav Republic of Macedonia, Montenegro, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Moldova, Albania, Ukraine and Georgia who still require a visa (holders of non-biometric passports), visa facilitation agreements stipulate a fee of EUR 35.00 ~ 1.100.000 VND.

## Service fee

[Table:  Tourist ]
Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in Vietnamese Dong (VND) is as per current exchange rate and will be updated later. It is subject to change without notice.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application Centre can pay the visa fee in cash or through credit / debit card.
- Applicants exempted from visa fee need to pay only VFS Global Service fee.
- The service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please refer to document checklist for your visa application here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/Besuch-VFS-Online-DEU-Mar-2026.pdf).
As a rule, applicants must submit visa applications, together with all necessary documents, in person at the Visa Application Centre responsible for their place of residence. In order to avoid time-consuming requests for additional information or documentation, applicants should consult the VFS’s websites and the website of the respective mission well in advance of their departure date to find out about the visa procedure and about the documentation which has to be submitted.
Visa application forms can be downloaded from the VFS’s website. The forms submitted must be original versions in the appropriate language of the mission in question.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Important note: 
- Photocopies that have been authenticated by Vietnamese government offices or notaries cannot be accepted for submission in lieu of original documents. However, it is permissible to submit, in lieu of originals, photocopies that have been recently (within the last 3 months) authenticated:
- by the German Embassy in Hanoi;
- by the German Consulate General in Ho Chi Minh City;
- by any German government office or
- by any Notary Public in Germany.
For a better understanding about the authentication process, please refer to the German Embassy and Consulate General’ website at https://vietnam.diplo.de/vn-vi/service/c%C3%B4ng-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-b%E1%BA%A3n-sao-v%C3%A0-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-th%E1%BB%B1c-ch%E1%BB%AF-k%C3%BD/2195386 (https://vietnam.diplo.de/vn-vi/service/c%C3%B4ng-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-b%E1%BA%A3n-sao-v%C3%A0-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-th%E1%BB%B1c-ch%E1%BB%AF-k%C3%BD/2195386)
- We strongly recommend the applicant to arrange your application per the checklist’s order, avoiding the delayed submission.
- Travel insurance:
- The policy is valid worldwide or in the Schengen (Schengen Area) or Europe;
- The policy covers the areas required by the visa code:
- Medical expenses (inpatient and outpatient)
- (Medical) evacuation
- Repatriation (of mortal remains)
- The insured sum for all these is equal to or higher than 30,000 EUR
- There is no sub-limit on these that is lower than 30,000 EUR.
Applicant is obliged to provide all information to the best of his or her knowledge and belief according to the regulation in Section 54 (2) 8 in conjunction with Section 53 of the Residence Act. Download form here and submit with the visa application form.
According to the Act on the General Freedom of Movement for EU Citizens for EU/EEA family members a simplified visa procedure applies. This does not apply for family members of German citizens. The application can be submitted either at the VFS Global Visa Application Centre or at the visa section of the German diplomatic representation. An appointment is not necessary, but highly recommended to avoid a long waiting period.
The following documents have to be submitted:
- Schengen Visa application form;
- Two biometric passport photos;
- Original passport;
- Additional information sheet pursuant Section 54 (2) 8 in conjunction with Section 53 of the Residence Act;
- Proof of relationship with the EU/EEA family member (for example marriage certificate in combination with a passport copy of the EU/EEA family member);
- Compulsory paid travel medical insurance.
The German diplomatic missions in Vietnam reserve the right to request additional supporting documents and/ or information if necessary. 

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
Photo specifications guideline (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/Photo-Specification-New.pdf)
Requirements:
- Should NOT be older than 6 months and without Photoshop touching;
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-36 mm) of the photo;
- A uniform and white-colored background;
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame;
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons;
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form.

## Processing Time
The processing of the application takes up to 15 working days.
The Embassy / the Consulate General of Germany in Vietnam may request further documentation, information or you may be called for an interview which can prolong the processing time.
After applying for your visa, you will not be able to borrow your passport back while your visa application is being processed.
If you need your passport during the visa process for other purposes, please inform us during the application process. You will have to hand in the passport at the same VfS-Center you applied as soon as possible to finalize the application process

## Download Forms
Below is the form that is required to be filled to submit your visa application:
- Schengen Visa Application Form (To be filled online - mandatory): The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled in English. Please click here (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/de/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt).
- Declaration of consent for minors can be downloaded here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/Declairation-Minor.pdf).
- Declaration on travel insurance if you want to apply for multiple entries visa can be downloaded here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/Declaration-on-travel-insurance-Visiting-friend-and-family.pdf).
- Declaration for visiting multiple Schengen countries can be downloaded here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/Declaration-for-visiting-multiple-Schengen-countries-Visiting-friend-and-family.pdf).
- Section 54 (2) 8 in conjunction with Section 53 of the Residence Act can be downloaded here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/Section-of-the-Residence-Act-Visiting-friend-and-family.pdf).
Instructions to complete an online form:
- Click on the form link above;
- When the page opens, complete the form. Attention points while filling the form:
- All the following fields are mandatory to fill up:
- Issued by;
- Duration of the intended stay or transit; Intended date of arrival in the Schengen area;
- Intended date of the departure from the Schengen area.
- For  the visiting friend and family visa category, the following  fields must be compulsory completed on VIDEX: 
- Surname and first name of the inviting person(s) in  the Member State(s). If not applicable, name of hotel (s)  or temporary accommodation(s) in the Member  State(s).
- Address and email of inviting person(s)/hotel(s)/temporary  accommodation(s).
- Telephone and fax of inviting  person(s)/hotel(s)/temporary accommodation(s).
- You should provide all the information, if  possible, on VIDEX, even if the field is not obligatory.
- Once complete print out the form on clear paper. It is recommended to save the application form under the .JSON file format and keep it on your email, allowing a quick and easy information correction or update if it requires.

## Overview
It is possible to get a visa with the purpose of taking part in Sports and Culture event. 
Please read carefully the following parts “Document requirement”, “Photo Specifications”, “Download Form” to properly prepare your application in conformity with the specific requirements of the Decision marker for this visa category.

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fee for the applicable visa type is listed in the table below. The fee mentioned is applicable per applicant. 

[Table:  Tourist ]
Fee waivers for Schengen visas:
Visa fees are waived by all Schengen states for the following categories of persons, regardless of their nationality:
- Children under six years.
- School pupils, undergraduates, postgraduates and accompanying teaching staff who undertake trips for the purpose of study or training.
- Representatives of non-profit organisations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations.
- Researchers from third countries travelling within the European Community for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in recommendation 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 September 2005 • For the “replacement” of an old, valid visa in a “full” travel document (one that has no empty pages) with a new visa valid for the same period in the applicant’s new travel document.
- Visa fees are waived for the spouses, same-sex partners in a civil union and minor single children of German citizens, the parents of minor German citizens and family members of EU/EEA nationals, provided they enjoy freedom of movement.
Reduced fees:
- The visa facilitation agreements with Armenia and Azerbaijan stipulate a fee of EUR 35.00 ~ 1.100.000 VND for a Schengen visa worldwide and various waivers of this visa fee (e.g. for visits to relatives).
- For those nationals of Serbia, the Former Yugoslav Republic of Macedonia, Montenegro, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Moldova, Albania, Ukraine and Georgia who still require a visa (holders of non-biometric passports), visa facilitation agreements stipulate a fee of EUR 35.00 ~ 1.100.000 VND.

## Service fee

[Table:  Tourist ]
Note: 
- The applicable visa fee in Vietnamese Dong (VND) is as per current exchange rate and will be updated later. It is subject to change without notice.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Visa Application Centre can pay the visa fee in cash or through credit / debit card.
- Applicants exempted from visa fee need to pay only VFS Global Service fee.
- The service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please refer to document checklist for you visa application here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/checklist-other-Categories-recent.pdf).
As a rule, applicants must submit visa applications, together with all necessary documents, in person at the Visa Application Centre responsible for their place of residence. In order to avoid time-consuming requests for additional information or documentation, applicants should consult the VFS’s websites and the website of the respective mission well in advance of their departure date to find out about the visa procedure and about the documentation which has to be submitted.
Visa application forms can be downloaded from the VFS’s website. The forms submitted must be original versions in the appropriate language of the mission in question.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/deu/travel-insurance)
Important note:
- Photocopies that have been authenticated by Vietnamese government offices or notaries cannot be accepted for submission in lieu of original documents. However, it is permissible to submit, in lieu of originals, photocopies that have been recently (within the last 3 months) authenticated: 
- by the German Embassy in Hanoi;
- by the German Consulate General in Ho Chi Minh City;
- by any German government office or
- by any Notary Public in Germany.
For a better understanding about the authentication process, please refer to the German Embassy and Consulate General’ website at https://vietnam.diplo.de/vn-vi/service/c%C3%B4ng-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-b%E1%BA%A3n-sao-v%C3%A0-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-th%E1%BB%B1c-ch%E1%BB%AF-k%C3%BD/2195386 (https://vietnam.diplo.de/vn-vi/dichvulanhsu/c%C3%B4ng-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-b%E1%BA%A3n-sao-v%C3%A0-ch%E1%BB%A9ng-th%E1%BB%B1c-ch%E1%BB%AF-k%C3%BD-2195386?isLocal=false&isPreview=false) 
- We strongly recommend the applicant to arrange your application per the checklist’s order, avoiding the delayed submission.
- Travel insurance:
- The policy is valid worldwide or in the Schengen (Schengen Area) or Europe;
- The policy covers the areas required by the visa code:
- Medical expenses (inpatient and outpatient)
- (Medical) evacuation
- Repatriation (of mortal remains)
- The insured sum for all these is equal to or higher than 30,000 EUR
- There is no sub-limit on these that is lower than 30,000 EUR.
Applicant is obliged to provide all information to the best of his or her knowledge and belief according to the regulation in Section 54 (2) 8 in conjunction with Section 53 of the Residence Act. Download form here (https://www.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/germany/vietnam/english/pdf/Download-form.pdf) and submit with the visa application form. 

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
Photo specifications guideline
Requirements:
- Should NOT be older than 6 months and without Photoshop touching;
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-36mm) of the photo;
- A uniform and white-colored background;
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame;
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons;
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form.

## Processing Time
The processing of the application takes up to 15 working days.
The Embassy / the Consulate General of Germany in Vietnam may request further documentation, information or you may be called for an interview which can prolong the processing time.
After applying for your visa, you will not be able to borrow your passport back while your visa application is being processed.
If you need your passport during the visa process for other purposes, please inform us during the application process. You will have to hand in the passport at the same VfS-Center you applied as soon as possible to finalize the application process

- Declaration for visiting multiple Schengen countries can be downloaded here.

- Intended date of the departure from the Schengen area.

- Once complete print out the form on clear paper. It is recommended to save the application form under the .JSON file format and keep it on your email, allowing a quick and easy information correction or update if it requires.
